entry.title: Re: SAP LGM problem with mobile applications
entry.content: Hi @SebastianLoeb, after the last Update of the Android Application the actual error gets propagated: Token was not set in the UserSecureStore. Any info's on that?

entry.title: Re: TDS amount not shown in supplier line item report
entry.content:
Why TDS shows in journal entry & TDS report but not in Manage Supplier line items is because, Supplier Line Item App (F0997A) is designed to show vendor open items and clearings based on supplier account postings. TDS postings are often made to tax accounts (with vendor line item cleared or adjusted separately). Depending on how the posting keys and account determination are set, the TDS component may not appear as a separate vendor line item. In contrast, the TDS report reads directly from withholding tax tables (e.g., BSET, BSEG), so it correctly shows the deduction. If the vendor line item is net of TDS, the gross vs. tax split may not be visible in the supplier line item report.

Having said that the system is working as designed and the TDS is correctly deducted and reflected in journal entries and tax reports. The “Manage Supplier Line Items” app does not always display TDS separately because those postings are made to tax accounts, not vendor accounts. If you need vendor-level visibility of TDS in the supplier line item report, you would need either:

  • A custom analytical report combining vendor line items with withholding tax tables, or
  • Use the Withholding Tax Items – India app for tax-specific reconciliation.

entry.title: Restriction on GL Account and Cost Center in Manage Service Entry Sheet App
entry.content:
Restriction on GL Account and Cost Center in Manage Service Entry Sheet App

Hello Experts,

We are trying to enforce restrictions on GL Account and Cost Center combinations in the Fiori Manage Service Entry Sheet (SES) app so that invalid combinations are blocked before the SES can be saved.

However, we observed that in the current SES processing flow, validation logic is not always executed before the data is persisted. In some cases, the validation error is raised after the data has already been committed, which allows restricted GL or Cost Center values to be saved.

We explored several options but faced limitations:

  • BAdI MM_PUR_S4_SES_CHECK_ITEM
    This BAdI provides header and item data but does not expose account assignment data (SESACCOUNTASSIGNMENT) where the GL and Cost Center information is stored.

  • BAdI FAGL_SEGMENT_BS_ACC
    This is triggered during FI posting, but not all GL accounts trigger it, making it unreliable for SES validation.

We also attempted the following:

  • Validation during SES creation, copy, and change scenarios

  • Reading CDS views

  • Calling FI_CHECK_ACCOUNT_ASSIGNMENT for GL + Cost Center validation

  • Extending account assignment data via the Custom Logic app

However, the available enhancement points in the SES flow do not consistently execute before the save, making it difficult to reliably block invalid combinations. Additionally, some relevant BAdIs appear to be SAP-internal and not open for customer implementation.

Our current framework version also does not expose the GL or Cost Center parameters that seem to exist in newer SES versions.

Has anyone successfully implemented a reliable validation or restriction for GL Account and Cost Center combinations in the Fiori SES app before saving?

Any guidance or recommended enhancement point would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: how to know open posting period by CDS view or API
entry.content:
Dear Vanessa,
What we need is simply to check the status of the accounting posting periods.
We believe that creating an actual journal entry in the production system just to verify whether a period is open or closed is too risky and impractical.
Is there any other way to verify if a specific posting period is open, other than attempting to post an actual document?
I look forward to expert's guidance.

Best regards,
Lina Kim.

entry.title: Re: Deep Dive: Essential Setup for S/4HANA and SAP Field Service Management Native Integration
entry.content:
Excellent detail walkthrough on the native integration between SAP S/4HANA and SAP Field Service Management (FSM).

Seamless data flow across ERP and front-line service operations is no longer optional—it’s the backbone of efficient service delivery. Real-time master data sync, unified asset management, and end-to-end service order processing directly translate into reduced manual effort, fewer errors, and empowered field technicians with accurate insights.

The detailed coverage of connectivity setup, OA2C authentication, DRF, AIF, MAIF, and FSM connector configuration makes this blog a valuable reference for consultants and architects driving integration projects.

BR, Dawid

https://me.sap.com/notes/3708420/E. 

Did you get a better alternative solution than to install the new DigiCert G5 certificate locally on each inidividual device of the affected users?

Thanks!
Michael

entry.title: Re: Tax rate not calculating for intergration with vertex...
entry.content:
Good day @Thiru2203 

Thank you for your questions.

Question: What could cause a migrated material to be treated as non-taxable by Vertex even though it appears to have similar attributes?

Answer: Based on your description, the issue you are facing is probably not the scheduling agreement itself, but a product master data difference between the material you created and the one you migrated.

The Product master migration object in Public Cloud includes a dedicated Tax Classifications section and for Tax Data (Sales) you must provide country, Tax category and Tax classification. Note that in Manage Product Master Data for a product master record, you should specify the Tax Classification for Sales Tax.

So, if you migrated Material B without the same tax classification values that exist on Material A, Vertex can receive the item with a different taxability interpretation and return it as non taxable.

Question: Which material fields are required for tax determination when using the Vertex tax engine?

Answer: For your scenario, the most relevant material fields you should check first to compare are the product’s sales tax classification fields in the sales area. So, you should ensure in your product master that:

  1. Sales area extension exists for the same sales org and distribution channel.
  2. You maintained Tax Classification for Sales Tax.
  3. In your migration terms, the product has the correct Country, Tax Category and Tax Classification in the Tax Classification section.

Consult these references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/289644d401a844878ce84670517dfa98.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/0483875792f25c08e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

Keep in mind that you can only provide tax classification data for a sales organisation that you newly create. It is not supported to update existing sales organisations in the migration object. This is important because a common root cause is:

  • The product was migrated,
  • The sales org extension exists, but the relevant tax classification data was not loaded correctly or was not loaded for the required sales org setup.

So, if you created Material B through migration and later extended differently, then you should check this restriction carefully.

Your duplicate text is very useful. Since your manually created product works, and a copy of that manual product also works, while the migrated product does not, that strongly points to a difference in master data difference on the product’s sales tax relevant data, not a general problem with Vertex connectivity, tax category O1, the scheduling agreement process itself or the overall tax procedure.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/0483875792f25c08e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

Please use Manage Product Master Data to compare the two products side by side and check at least in the:

  • Sales area (same sales organisation, same distribution channel and same Tax Classification for Sales Tax)
  • In your migration content (same country, same tax category and same tax classification in the Tax Classifications sheet or structure).
  • In your organisational setup, confirm the migrated product is actually extended to the same sales area in which you are creating the scheduling agreement.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/9c62e3f7092744d49021051301263e85.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Allow only managers to request classes.
entry.content: Hi @carolinafaria The "Access Registrations" permission, part of the My Team permission ID with default user role, allows managers to manage enrollment for their subordinates in Team View. With this permission, managers can use the "Manage Enrollment" option to register and withdraw employees from classes.

entry.title: Priority of inspection plan
entry.content:
Hello,

I have two inspection plans for incoming inspection, Plan 1 and Plan 2. The only difference between these two inspection plan is that I set Dynamic mod. level as Dynamic Modification at Char. Level for Plan 2 and no DMR for Plan 1. I'm wondering after good receipt, how SAP system define which inspection plan to trigger for inspection lot creating? I would prefer to create inspection lot using Plan 2 which set DMR, is there any method to improve Plan 2's priority? Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Performance Form Business Rule Returni...
entry.content: Thank you Jordi, My business rule is for the Role Readiness form. The requirement is: if the rating given by the employee and the rating given by the manager differ by more than 2 points, then the comments field should become mandatory.

entry.title: Re: Questions : March 2026 Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers in ABAP
entry.content: Hi, I did that and waited for 4 hrs but still facing the same issue.

entry.title: Re: Refill Plan in S/4HANA Cross-Catalog Mapping
entry.content: In S 4HANA the Refill Plan is usually not assigned through Cross-Catalog Mapping like it was in CRM. The refill or recurring logic is typically handled through SAP Convergent Charging. You need to configure the refill behavior in Convergent Charging and link it to the subscription products charge or rate plan. The subscription product then uses that charging configuration to manage the refill cycle.

entry.title: Re: Beyond Manual: SuccessFactors Custom MDF & Integration Center for Automated Reimbursement Pr
entry.content: Hi @Islem_Jendoubi . Great blog. I just have one question regarding the integration. Is scheduled the only way to trigger the job? is there any possibility where the job is triggered as soon as the record gets approved in the custom mdf?

entry.title: BW to BDC migration for large BW systems exceeding BW PCE size limit
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

What options are available for large SAP BW systems e.g. exceeding 30 TB when transitioning to BDC? Due to current BW PCE size limit it is not possible to simply lift such a system to PCE. Is the only reasonable scenario to gradually rebuild BW data flows in Datasphere and retain BW on-prem?

BR, Dawid

entry.title: Re: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty...
entry.content: Continue discussing with SAP Support because their first answers are usually very general. I'm surprised that an error text mentioning "table ARFCRSTATE" is not first a problem at SAP side.

entry.title: Re: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty...
entry.content:
I have never seen this error, and I've been working with IDocs and tRFC for 27 years...

Doesn't look like an NCo error. I detailed RFC trace might help. Could be there is something broken on tRFC protocol level. (BizTalk SAP Adapter is on the latest patch level? I know there were quite a few bugs in earlier versions with regards to IDoc handling.)

entry.title: Re: Week 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account enabled with Joule for Developers

entry.title: Hello SAP community! My name is Shlok Shigli and I'm excited to start my journey
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community Post – Shlok Shigli

Introduction

Hello SAP Community! 👋

My name is Shlok Shigli, and I am currently pursuing Mechanical Engineering at REVA University in Bangalore, India.

I’m excited to be part of the Hello SAP Community initiative. This opportunity allows beginners like me to explore the SAP ecosystem, learn from experienced professionals, and connect with people across the global SAP network.

As a student, I am passionate about learning new technologies and understanding how SAP solutions help businesses manage operations more efficiently. Through the SAP Community, I look forward to gaining insights from experts, discovering new ideas, and building meaningful professional connections.

I’m excited to share my learning journey and contribute to the SAP Community as I continue to grow.

Thank you, SAP Community, for this wonderful opportunity!

1. What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to build a successful career at the intersection of technology and engineering. I want to continuously learn new skills and work on innovative solutions that help organizations improve efficiency and make better decisions. I also hope to contribute to the tech community and grow as a professional in the global technology ecosystem.

2. Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In the next five years, I see myself as a skilled technology professional with strong technical knowledge and hands-on experience. I hope to work on real-world projects, continuously improve my expertise, and contribute positively to my organization and the wider technology community.

3. What is your hobby?

One of my hobbies is exploring new technologies and learning about innovations in engineering and business systems. I enjoy reading, learning new skills online, and continuously expanding my knowledge through different learning platforms.

4. Which 5 things do you absolutely want to achieve in life?

  • Build a strong career in the technology and engineering field
  • Continuously improve myself by learning new technologies and skills
  • Work on projects that create real-world impact
  • Build meaningful connections and collaborate with talented people
  • Maintain a balanced life while pursuing personal and professional growth

5. What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

One of the most important lessons my parents taught me is to stay disciplined and never stop learning. They always reminded me that progress takes time and consistency. Their guidance has helped me stay focused on my goals and approach challenges with patience and determination.

My Experience at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru

SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2026 – Excited to be part of this amazing SAP Community eventSAP campus in Bengaluru

Recently, I had the opportunity to attend SAP Inside Track Bengaluru, and it was an incredibly valuable learning experience for me as a student exploring the SAP ecosystem.

Being part of an event where SAP professionals and community members shared their knowledge was very inspiring. It helped me better understand how SAP technologies are used to solve real-world business problems and improve organizational efficiency.

One of the most interesting parts of the event was interacting with SAP experts and fellow learners. Listening to their experiences and insights gave me a broader perspective on the different opportunities within the SAP ecosystem.

An insightful session on Generative AI and AI Agents,

Key Takeaways from the Event

  • The growing importance of AI and intelligent technologies in enterprise solutions
  • How SAP platforms help organizations improve efficiency and business processes
  • The strong role of the SAP Community in sharing knowledge and supporting learners.

I’m excited to continue learning and sharing my journey with the SAP Community as I explore more about SAP technologies

entry.title: Re: Poland Tax Repporting date correction process with KSEF
entry.content: Thank you for your feedback again. In general in Poland, what is the legitimate way to do "value-corrections" ? We are trying to choose one of the 2 ways: A. Credit memo with just difference (incase of overcharge) B. Sales doc type "RK-Invoice Correct. Req"

entry.title: Talent Intelligence Hub - how to handle custom attribute types that don’t require proficiency scales
entry.content:
We’re implementing the Talent Intelligence Hub and have several custom attribute types (e.g., Projects, Certificates) where a proficiency scale is not relevant.

However, it appears that TIH requires a proficiency level scale to be configured for every attribute type (otherwise error occurs) As a result, we must create a “dummy” scale even for attributes where proficiency does not conceptually apply.

Has anyone found a clean workaround for this requirement? For example, using a 1‑point scale, a placeholder label, or another recommended approach?

entry.title: Re: Community SMP Minecraft Server
entry.content: I’d love a world download too, just to walk around the old builds again. If it’s possible to share it before shutdown, I’d really appreciate it.

entry.title: Re: Issues Related to Down Payment in Purchase Order
entry.content:
Hello @chhatrapal_mahajanacc82e,

Please see below some answers to your questions:

1) When I add a down payment at the header level in the Purchase Order, the amount is not reflecting while creating the Down Payment Request.

By “the amount is not reflecting”, do you mean that the amount is not proposed while you are creating the Down Payment Request? If yes, this is the expected system behavior, please see here: 2894701 - Down payment amount in PO header not proposed when creating Down Payment Request - SAP for Me. If you want to have a proposed value in "Create Down Payment Request" you have to define the down payment on item level in the PO.

2) If a down payment of ₹10 is maintained in the PO, the system is still allowing the creation of a Down Payment Request for more than ₹10.

Because of the behaviour described under 1 the down payment value maintained at the PO header does not control, limit, or validate the down payment request amount. Therefore, the system also allows the Down Payment Request to exceed the amount maintained at header level.

3) When I use the “Monitor Purchase Order Down Payment Requests” tile, the workflow is not getting triggered/working.

When you use Monitor Purchase Order Down Payments you can use the button “Create Down Payment Request”, which should navigate you to the Manage Supplier Down Payment Requests app. If this navigation is not working, it could be due to some missing authorization or due to a system error. In this case, opening a case in https://me.sap.com/home might be helpful for you.

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: How to call a custom FM once after all changed line items in VA01/VA02?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m working on VA01/VA02 and have a requirement:

  • The user can change the item category for multiple line items.

  • After the user edits the line items and presses Enter, I need to pass all the changed line items to a custom function module to show a popup.

  • The popup should be called only once, regardless of how many line items were changed.

  • I do not want the popup to wait until the user clicks Save.

I’ve tried using USEREXIT_CHECK_VBAP and USEREXIT_MOVE_FIELD_TO_VBAP, but the FM gets triggered multiple times (once per line item).

Is there a standard exit or best practice to detect “all line items have been changed” so I can call my FM only once before Save? Or is the memory table + flag approach the recommended solution?

Thanks in advance for any guidance!

Thanks and regards,

Praveen de Silva.

entry.title: Document Grounding - Error when trying to get bearer token with Bruno
entry.content:

We are setting up Joule Document Grounding following the guide here:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/joule-getting-started-with-document-grounding-setup-guide-1/bc-p/14347572#M189016
Looks like everything is configured correctly but we are getting this error when trying to get the bearer token using Bruno:

any hint please?
thanks,
Marco

entry.title: Re: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty...
entry.content: We are using WCF SAP NCO adapter in Biztalk and version is 3.1.6. I predict its compatibility issue at S4-Biztalk.Below is latest update from SAP

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Dino C D and I'm a Cybersecurity Graduate with a Passion for IoT, AI
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

My name is Dino C D and I'm a postgraduate student pursuing my MSc in Artificial Intelligence & Cybersecurity at Christ University, BYC, Bengaluru. I completed my Bachelor of Computer Applications at St Joseph's University, Bengaluru, where I built a strong foundation in programming, cloud deployment, full-stack development, and IoT engineering.

 

Beyond academics, I'm a multi-award-winning innovator (3× 1st Prize for my IoT IV Blood Detector Device), a patent applicant, an active cybersecurity community contributor through S.H.I.E.L.D, and a researcher working on audio deepfake detection. I love building things that solve real problems — whether that's a smart medical device, an interactive history website, or a security-aware web application.

Pitching my patent-pending IoT IV Blood Detector to a C-DAC judge

 

What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to become a leading cybersecurity engineer who doesn't just defend systems — but actively shapes the way security is built into technology from the ground up. I want to work at the intersection of AI and cybersecurity, developing intelligent threat detection systems that can anticipate attacks before they happen.

On a bigger scale, I want to contribute to making healthcare technology safer. My IoT IV Blood Detector project opened my eyes to how vulnerable critical medical infrastructure can be. My dream is to see a world where hospital systems, patient data, and medical IoT devices are protected by design — and I want to be part of building that future. A patent in progress feels like the first small step toward that dream.

 

 

 

 

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In five years, I see myself working as a SOC Analyst or Cybersecurity Researcher at a technology or cybersecurity firm — ideally contributing to threat intelligence, incident response, or AI-powered security tooling. I hope to have completed my MSc, earned certifications like CompTIA Security+ and CEH, and published at least one peer-reviewed research paper (starting with my current work on audio deepfake detection).

I also see myself mentoring the next wave of cybersecurity students — much like the mentors and communities that shaped me. If the journey goes the way I hope, perhaps I'll be sharing a very different blog post on the SAP Community in 2031, looking back at how it all started. 😊

 

Why did you choose MSc Artificial Intelligence & Cybersecurity at Christ University?

Honestly, it felt like the perfect convergence of everything I was already passionate about. After my BCA, I knew I wanted to go deeper — not just into cybersecurity, but into how AI is reshaping the entire threat landscape. Christ University's programme offered exactly that combination.

What truly won me over was the learning environment. The workshops have been eye-opening — hands-on, practical, and constantly pushing you to think beyond textbooks. The classes don't just teach concepts, they challenge you to question, build, and apply. Every session feels like it's preparing you for a problem that actually exists in the real world.

I'm genuinely grateful for the exposure this course has given me — from AI-driven security techniques to research opportunities. It's not just a degree; it's the foundation I needed to become the cybersecurity professional I want to be. 🎓


What was the most fun project you ever participated in and why?

Without a doubt: Temporal Explorer — Past to Future.

The idea was simple but ambitious — build an interactive website that lets users travel through time, from ancient civilisations all the way to speculative futures. We used HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to create animated timelines, artefact showcases, and immersive storytelling experiences.

What made it so fun wasn't just the tech — it was the creativity involved. We debated what the future might look like, researched historical artefacts, and designed transitions that felt like you were genuinely moving through eras. Completing it in time for the Web Weavers intercollegiate competition and walking away with 2nd place made it even more rewarding. It reminded me that the best projects aren't just technically sound — they tell a story.


What is your hobby?

My biggest hobby is community work and volunteering. Over the past few years, I've coordinated 50+ outreach events with AICUF, taught constitutional literacy in government schools through KJES, and helped build the cybersecurity awareness culture at Christ University through S.H.I.E.L.D.

It might sound unusual as a "hobby," but there's something deeply energising about connecting with people, solving real-world problems together, and seeing the direct impact of your effort — whether that's a student who finally understands their civic rights, or a community that feels a little more supported. It keeps me grounded in why I'm pursuing technology in the first place: to serve people.

A volunteering Social work which was held in Biccodu, Karnataka by KJES

 


What do you imagine or predict is the future of your topic — AI & Cybersecurity?

The future is fascinating — and a little terrifying, honestly. Here's my prediction:

AI will become the most powerful weapon in both attack and defence simultaneously. We're already seeing AI-generated phishing emails that are indistinguishable from real ones, deepfake audio used in fraud (which is exactly why I'm researching it), and autonomous malware that adapts in real time. The adversarial AI race is already here.

On the defence side, I believe AI-powered SOC platforms will eventually handle 90% of threat triage autonomously — flagging only the most complex incidents for human analysts. Security will shift from "detect and respond" to "predict and prevent."

The wildcard? IoT security. Billions of connected devices — many with zero security baked in — will be the biggest attack surface of the next decade. My IV detector project taught me first-hand how exposed medical IoT can be. I believe the next generation of cybersecurity professionals will spend as much time securing physical devices as they do securing data.

The future belongs to those who understand both the intelligence of AI and the vulnerabilities it creates.


Share a fun fact about yourself that people don't know!

I once helped coordinate a community event managing ₹2.5 lakh in organisational expenditure — and I was a second-year undergrad student at the time. 

As the core committee member for CHRONICLES (AICUF's major fest at St Joseph's University), I was suddenly responsible for budgeting, resource allocation, vendor coordination, and keeping everything on track. No one tells you that community leadership is basically a crash course in project management, crisis handling, and people skills — all at once.

Oh, and I did all of that while simultaneously building AWS deployments, learning Kali Linux, and developing IoT devices in my spare time. Sleep was optional, apparently.

With Justice Kurian Joseph, Retired Judge of the Supreme Court of India — Chief Guest at CHRONICLES, St Joseph's University, Bengaluru. ⚖️

 

And now let's hear from you!

What do you imagine or predict is the future of AI and Cybersecurity — and how are you preparing for it? Drop your thoughts in the comments below — I'd love to hear where you think the field is heading! 🚀

Dino C D | MSc AI & Cybersecurity Student, Christ University, Bengaluru
🔗linkedin.com/in/dinocd | 💻 github.com/Dino20004

Matthias

entry.title: Re: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty...
entry.content: You can find my SAP reply. I also change idoc segment release from 758 to 701 but no luck. Also cleared all SMQ1, SMQ2 and SM58 entries no luck too. Thinking of clean up Clean up all old unwanted memory dumps, entries in arfcstate and arfcdata. Programs RSARFCER, RSTRFCES , RSSNAPDL, RSARFCDL, RSTRFCEF, RSTRFCEO, RSTRFCQD, TRFC_LUW_FINISH. Also Check the lockwaits and deadlocks in ST04/DB6COCKPIT.

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Community! My name is Dino C D and I'm a Cybersecurity Graduate with a Passion for IoT
entry.content:
Hi @Dino_24-2004 ,

Great post and thank you for sharing! Do you think the "defenders" will have a significant disadvantage in future cyberattacks in that they will be more bound by ethics and "human-in-the-loop" policies versus attackers who will be more than willing to set loose a fully autonomous attack agent to wreak havoc? 

Further - do you see this disadvantage leading to defenders ultimately giving full autonomy to AI cybersecurity agents in order to keep up?

entry.title: Re: ABAP Restful, annotation for navigaton with condition
entry.content:
Maybe you can dynamically construct url by using "CASE WHEN".

e.g.,  '/sap/bc/ui2/flp#Order-display'、'/sap/bc/ui2/flp#Contract-display'.

Then use the following annotation.

@ui.LineItem: [{ type: #WITH_URL, url: 'url' }]

entry.title: Re: SAP Job Scheduling Service: We Redesigned the Dashboard (And Added Analytics)
entry.content:
Hi @Cocquerel,
Thanks I think I get it now, and your setup is pretty common. One idea we have is to have separate BTP Roles for the Job Scheduling service (we have that for instances consumed on Kyma) like `SAP_Job_Scheduling_Service_Admin` and `SAP_Job_Scheduling_Service_Viewer`which you can assign to a user without giving them CloudFoundry "space developer" roles. The technical limitation would be that these roles won't allow the creation of management of CF Tasks.

How does this sound to you? Would this be a viable alternative keeping in mind the technical limitations of this approach?

entry.title: Re: IDOCs are Still Safe for SAP S/4HANA - SAP Clean Core Extensibility Level B
entry.content:
@MichalKrawczyk @signortintin @GaganKumar 

But adding  Z enhancements is still Level B Clean core? My concern is on it very high.

Because we have IDOCs enhancements in SAP ECC now when we move to SAP S4 Private Cloud we don't wanted to disturb existing mappings in middleware.

Please let me know your inputs. Incase if we go wiht BTP route the latency is too high

 

SAP S4 <-> SAP BTP <-> Boomi <-> SFTP <-> WAN <-> EDI Partners

entry.title: Re: IDOCs are Still Safe for SAP S/4HANA - SAP Clean Core Extensibility Level B
entry.content:
We are in tight situation that we cannot change IDOC to APIs considering several dimensions and dependencies. Since existing SAP ECC have some custom fields for few partners and we don't wanted to disturb existing mappings in middleware.

As is -   200 + EDI Partners <-> MS Biztalk  <-> SAP ECC

To be - 200 + EDI Partners <-> MS Biztalk  <-> SAP S4 - Utilize same enchanced IDOC to minizmei code changes in biztalk for faster delivery

Also is it possible to enhance IDOCs in SAP BTP? like :

200 + EDI Partners <-> MS Biztalk  <-> SAP BTP(idoc extensions) <-> SAP S4 (clean core)

Thankyou!!

entry.title: SAP ARIBA: GRN Receipt Number still showing in ariba business network even invoice is parked
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community, 

I am facing an issue where the GRN receipt number is still showing in Ariba Business Network even when the invoice has been parked (the receipt number should have been consumed). The invoice status was rejected in the business network however it is parked in SAP.

SAP Ariba Contracts  SAP Ariba Invoicing SAP Ariba Sourcing SAP Ariba Sourcing

entry.title: Re: Max Success Forums
entry.content: @helenemeyer I am struggling to find any blog post or event on the Max Success Plan Events page that provide links to register. Can you please point me to the right page?

entry.title: Re: 1010 Balance Carryforward - Divestured Consolidation Unit's Reserves are not BCF
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Proposal for Product XXXX Plant XXXX MRP Area XXXX ca...
entry.content:
@M_Samiullah Please check my accepted solution https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-q-a/proposal-for-product-xxxx-plant-xxxx-mrp-area-xxxx-can-t-be-adopted-in/qaa-p/13860362/highlight/true#M200125

It suggests the note 3490869 - DDMRP: Descriptive error messages for buffer proposal rejections. The note adds more descriptive messages in case a proposal can't be adopted. Hope this helps you resolving the problem.

entry.title: Re: it is possible to create a purchase order using a div...
entry.content:
Good day @arnaudf 

Thank you for your questions.

The key reason why the plant is blocked is, plant and division are treated differently in the organisational model. Sales structures are linked to Finance through company code and to procurement through plant, while division belongs to the Sales and Distribution organisational setup.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/88d0329957b5473e95880e1c09880e3c.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c473e4fb3bca4420badc0de89c520328/ef0a6270dae145d68340cadf9e31f031-26.html?locale=en-US

Because of this, when you change the company code in your purchase order, the system performs a consistency validation for procurement relevant data such as plant, which is why the plant mismatch is blocked. I am not aware of any hard validation for division against company code when you save PO, which explains why you can still save the document in your test.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0864cb07010642b3bde45a20de4975bc/2187ec203ad74f578f2aa2e6cf3accad.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/38cbf557c328be12e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

So, your result is system driven rather than an application error. If your business requires a stricter check for division after company code change, that would be an additional requirement rather than something covered by the standard PO validation logic.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Subaccount id in BTP & challenges in CALM
entry.content: Hi @RajaSubramaniyan, I'm not sure whether I get your question correctly. From Change & Deployment Management perspective it is not a problem to have several Subaccounts to deploy development artifacts to. I don't understand the point about transporting same code with modifications. Can you clarify this a bit? Thanks, Moritz

entry.title: How can I access the List Inquiries App (VA15) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
How can I access the List Inquiries App (VA15) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How can I access the List Inquiries App (VA15) in SAP...
entry.content:
To access the app 

Open The Manage Sales Inquiry App > Select an Inquiry > In the top left hand corner click the Menu drop down > Go To Environment > Go to List > Select Inquiry.

The App will now be displayed. 

*Please ensure the relevant roles have been assigned to the user as per the configuration section set out in the Fiori Apps Library https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('VA15')/S36

entry.title: Re: Accessing Exam
entry.content: Thank you Jordi. I actually relaised this was the wrong place to post this. However, I created a case with exam support and eventually it worked for me.. Took a couple of days though. And it only seemed to apply to the Learning Exam.

entry.title: Unable to access Compliance Documents or request authorization
entry.content:
I am unable to access any restricted content in SAP for Me — including Compliance Documents, invoices, balance statements, and agreements. All cards show "Restricted Card Content."

Please suggest which authorization is being used here and how to get the authorization. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to access Compliance Documents or request auth...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Kindly know the required S-user authorization to access or download the compliance documents is: Access Compliance Documents. Refer to KBA 3629697.

Please kindly request the missing authorization using the self-service authorization request feature as introduced in KBA 2891528 - How to use the Self-Service Authorization Request - SAP for Me.

Hope this helps.

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty...
entry.content:
I meant the BizTalk version, not the NCo version. Make sure to have the latest BizTalk patches.

And also: according to SAP Support's latest reply, you are still using NCo 3.0.22 (which has been out of support for a few years already - why such an old version?!). So the update to 3.1.6 did not work correctly.

entry.title: Re: How to Add Custom Button in BP Application ToolBar
entry.content: Hi BabuGunturu, Can you please let me know how did your issue resolve? Were you able to acheive custom button and also to display general data and company code buttons? I am facing the same issue.

entry.title: Re: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty...
entry.content:
I think, it cannot be a problem on SAP side, because if I understand the scenario correctly, then SAP is the sender, and BizTalk the receiver, right? ARFCRSTATE is used on receiver side ("R"), while if it were a problem on sender side, the table would be ARFCSSTATE ("S" for sender).

Also SM58 usually displays the error message coming back from the receiver side (BizTalk/NCo), so it looks like the error is happening there. As I said: the RFC trace on BizTalk/NCo side should show more details.

Thinking a bit more about it, something like this must be happening: SAP sends a tRFC (which includes the State table, which in turn contains the transaction ID (TID)). But when the call arrives on NCo side, NCo notices that that table is empty. So either it is indeed a problem on SAP side (SAP accidentally sending empty/incorrect transaction data), or another idea that just comes to my mind: is the SM59 destination used for sending these IDocs set to "Non-Unicode"? This could cause all kinds of data corruption problems. (And this could also be seen in an RFC trace...)

If this is the case, set it to "Unicode", delete all old IDocs in SM58 (because they have been recorded in Non-Unicode format) and then try a fresh IDoc.

entry.title: Re: it is possible to create a purchase order using a div...
entry.content: Hello Chris, I feel sorry, in my description I talk only about Plant (= division in French), so it confusing. I would like to change the initial message but I can't! So only MM structure organisational elements, no SD. Thank you

entry.title: Re: Joule integration with SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite – Setup Guide
entry.content:
@stafenthomas  .  at point 5 in standard documentation. It says

  1. Create a new service binding with the name of your choice in INTEGRATION - Instances and Subscriptions - Instances  Joule-Signavio-DS. 

There is no instance with name created by the booster. 

Thanks

Amit

entry.title: Re: 1010 Balance Carryforward - Divestured Consolidation...
entry.content:
Good day @FranConde 

Thank you for your questions.

Based on SAP’s standard design, the system does not provide separate automatic task that will carry forward reserves of a divested consolidation unit as opening balances of that same divested unit in the following year.

The following are the reasons that happens:

  • First, balance Carryforward pull balances of relevant FS items from the previous fiscal year into the current fiscal year. Which also confirms LEQ and AST FS items being always carried forward as a business requirement.
  • Second, total Divestiture is used to invert all data previously posted in the consolidation of investments for the divested investee. So, only balance sheet or statistical items are reversed and protected items such as retained earnings are treated via the consolidation of investments clearing logic.
  • Third, for loss of control/total divestiture, the system does not undo consolidation group-dependent entries.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/86b7e4024e4b4264891ce4d377ca075c.html?locale=en-US

In summary, this means the system does not automatically interpret “reserves that should remain in group equity after divestiture” as “opening balances that should stay on the divested consolidation unit next year”. So, in standard design, once the unit is divested, the expectation should usually be that those amounts are either:

  1. Reversed as part of divestiture logic or
  2. Explicitly retained at group level by reclassification, not left on the divested unit as its new-year opening balance.

 

Question: Are you missing any any automatic task or setup that manages this point?

Answer: No additional task is required for this exact point. The standard relevant tasks are:

  • For you to maintain the divestiture in the consolidation hierarchy
  • Then run the appropriate Total Divestiture/consolidation group change processing in the divestiture year
  • And finally run Balance Carryforward for the year change.

 

Considering there is none, the best practice for this point is:

  • For you not to expect the divested consolidation unit itself to carry those reserves forward into the next year once it has left the group. That is not the standard conceptual target of BCF after divestiture.
  • If your reserves from realised profits must remain in group equity, then the better practice is for you to reclassify them into a surviving group equity position rather than leave them on the divested unit.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/86b7e4024e4b4264891ce4d377ca075c.html?locale=en-US

  • So, in practice, this would usually mean the following:
    • Complete the divestiture processing in the year of divestment
    • Analyse which balance must remain in consolidated equity
    • Post a manual Group Reporting journal entry (using Import Group Journal Entries) to move the relevant reserve balance to the appropriate parent or group-level FS item before or after year-end, depending on your accounting design and reporting timeline.

So, the likely best practice is for you to manually reclassify your reserve to the continuing group structure, not expecting BCF to keep it on the divested unit.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/a2d5433ad6b045c99b2ec8142b09f097.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: When will the 'As of Date' calculation be available in Story similar to Canvas?
entry.content: The canvas 'As of Date' calculation can be added to the canvas report output columns, which is very convenient for report consumers to know what 'as of' data is in the data download especially if they are comparing multiple spreadsheets. When will this calculation or field be available in story reports?

entry.title: Sequence priority mass changes in routing

Dear all,

 

Please help me how can I change the sequence priority in routing? I tried the CEWB transaction, but I didn't find an option to solve it.

Best regards,

Attila

entry.title: 🇵🇱 SAP HANA Cloud for AI Applications: ML, Vectors, and Knowledge Graphs (Poznań, Poland)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event focuses on SAP HANA Cloud for AI Applications, covering topics such as machine learning, Vectors, and Knowledge Graphs. You don't need any background in the topic, but you should be highly curious and have some basic programming skills!

This is a free in-person event planned for Wednesday, April 22, in Poznań, Poland 🇵🇱 from 10:00 to 16:00 local time. The event's language and content will be in English. 

The event is hosted by Capgemini at
Business Garden Poznań
ul. Kolorowa 6
60-198 Poznań
1st floor, room Borneo

Please note that space is limited; we can accommodate only 25 attendees.

By the end of the session, you’ll have built examples that demonstrate how SAP HANA Cloud can serve as a back-end for modern Artificial Intelligence applications:

  • Leverage the SAP HANA Vector Engine for business information embeddings—building a vector store that supports semantic similarity search.
  • Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)  to enrich generative AI outputs with precise, up-to-date data.
  • Model and Query a Knowledge Graph inside SAP HANA using RDF triples to extract semantic relationships.
  • Integrate HANA with LangChain to build AI apps that orchestrate prompt workflows, execute database statements, and navigate knowledge graphs—all directly from Python , in this case

entry.title: Re: SAP LGM problem with mobile applications
entry.content: Can you please let me know where you see this error? On the Mobile Device or in the Mobile Services logs? Are you trying to use single sign on with client certificates on the Mobile Device?

entry.title: Re: Week 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account enabled with Joule for Developers
entry.content:
Week 1 completed!!
Package ZAC_2286263.

entry.title: Re: Issues Related to Down Payment in Purchase Order
entry.content: Thanks for your valuable response.

entry.title: Re: HTTP failed: INTERNAL_ERROR in SAP Gateway Client for...
entry.content: Hello, The issue was not related to the CDS view or OData configuration. In my case the problem was caused by the host name used by the Gateway Client. The system tried to call http://sap:8000/..., but the host sap was not resolvable (no DNS/hosts entry). Because of this, the request failed before reaching the Gateway service. Calling the service with localhost solved the issue. Example: Incorrect: http://sap:8000/sap/opu/odata/sap/<service_name>/$metadata Working: http://localhost:8000/sap/opu/odata/sap/<service_name>/$metadata I was testing a CDS OData service created in the $TMP package.

entry.title: Re: Test, Demo, and Development: Discovery Package "How‑to" Hub for SAP Cloud ERP, Private
entry.content:
Hi @FlorentTardivon 

Local JAVA VM ADS service is not included in RISE Discovery.
Instead users can use the  BTP‑based ADS (which is probably what you already mean by pointing to SAP forms by Adobe), see here for info: https://help.sap.com/docs/forms-service-by-adobe/sap-forms-service-cf/configure-sap-forms-service-in-s-4hana-on-premise-systems

best, Bettina

entry.title: Re: Refill Plan in S/4HANA Cross-Catalog Mapping
entry.content: Hello satyamahuja, thank you for your response. In our current CRM setup, we also have a charge plan so both Charge Plan and Refill Plan are assigned in the product's Cross-Catalog Mapping. In SAP Convergent Charging, I believe that we can't directly enter a Refill Plan in a Charge Plan as they are separate catalog objects. Do you mean that we should re-create the refill behavior within the Charge Plan without using Refill Objects? kind regards, kc

entry.content:
Week 2 completed!!
Package ZAC_2286263.

entry.title: Re: How you can manage Polish KSeF Requirement in SAP S/⁠4HANA Cloud Private Edition & SAP S/⁠4H
entry.content:
Dear all

is there a solution available to display the ODN-number in the line item in RFPOEXT-structure (like the KSEF-number in RFPOSEXT)

Best Regards

Birgit

entry.title: Re: Enable Offline Store for Online OData Service in SAP MDK
entry.content:
To add to Bills comment, You should be careful with trying to store or combine data across services. Data from the online service does not automatically get saved into the offline DB, and records cannot be "linked" across destinations/services. Ideally if you want something offline, you can use the Online search to find the object and then check the object key into the dependancy queue (for SSAM) for the offline service to then download this object. then the user can interact with the object in offline mode. The offline and online structure & data should be mirrored so that inconsistencies do not arise.

Most of the Data in SSAM online service is also available in the offline Service. SSAM is offline first by design. So you should have access to the Data offline then options to use the online search for additional data, which can then be downloaded. (Equip, Floc, unplanned parts, Goods movemnts etc.. )

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRVHello, I am integrating Supplier Invoice da
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I am integrating Supplier Invoice data using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using API odatav2 API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV.

 

While trying to post the supplier invoice, in some cases, i am facing problemas related to unit measure when using the navigation to_SuplrInvcItemPurOrdRef.

 

Here a payload example on this navigation:

 

<d:SupplierInvoiceItem>1</d:SupplierInvoiceItem>

<d:PurchaseOrder>100002268</d:PurchaseOrder>

<d:PurchaseOrderItem>50</d:PurchaseOrderItem>

<d:SupplierInvoiceItemAmount>6460</d:SupplierInvoiceItemAmount>

<d:QuantityInPurchaseOrderUnit>1</d:QuantityInPurchaseOrderUnit>

<d:SupplierInvoiceItemText>Test</d:SupplierInvoiceItemText>

<d:PurchaseOrderQuantityUnit>VG</d:PurchaseOrderQuantityUnit>

<d:TaxCode>R1</d:TaxCode>

<d:DocumentCurrency>EUR</d:DocumentCurrency>

<d:ReferenceDocument>5000020947</d:ReferenceDocument>

<d:ReferenceDocumentItem>5</d:ReferenceDocumentItem>

<d:ReferenceDocumentFiscalYear>2026</d:ReferenceDocumentFiscalYear>

 

When sending this payload sap returns me the following error:

No measurement unit is assigned to ISO code SPACE

 

What could be wrong here? Do i need to send the ISO and SAP code? Why on some cases it works and others dont?

 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Discovery Package: Test, Demo, and Development for SAP Cloud ERP Private
entry.content:
Hi @sami1982 

Yes, reprovisioning gives you a new system, from the original template or a newer version as selected by you. You need to reapply the customizing, e.g. by using gCTS. 

We have no utilities content in the system, it gives you S/4HANA core only. You can check the Getting started guide on the landing page for a list of all installed components.

best regards, Bettina

entry.title: Career Launchpad for the SAP Ecosystem
entry.content:
This 6-day intensive program provides participants with essential SAP ecosystem orientation, career pathway navigation, and fundamental hands-on experience—creating a strong foundation for enterprise technology careers. Designed as a comprehensive launcher program, it introduces students to SAP's global landscape, available learning resources, and certification pathways while building practical skills through real project work.

Check out all the details in the program overview -  https://gamma.app/docs/Moldova-Career-Launchpad-for-the-SAP-Ecosystem-aexyz4w8dw29iuf?mode=doc

entry.title: Issue with Delivery Creation Date Calculation in PO
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am currently facing an issue while creating a Purchase Order (PO). Even though the delivery date is properly maintained in the PO, the system is calculating the delivery creation date as a past date based on the planned delivery time.

Normally, the delivery creation date should be calculated as PO creation date + planned delivery time, resulting in a future date. However, in this case, it appears that the system is calculating it as PO creation date – planned delivery time.

Due to this behavior, users are encountering difficulties while creating outbound deliveries through VL10D, as the PO is not appearing in the list. This seems to be because the delivery date in the system is being updated as a past date.

Could anyone please help identify the reason for this behavior and suggest how this issue can be resolved?

Your support would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Manage Purchase Contracts App – Output Details Greyed...
entry.content: Appreciate and Thank you for your thought, Unfortunately, access to the GUI apps has been disabled. That is why I am asking within the Manage Purchase Contract app itself.

entry.title: Re: Test, Demo, and Development: Discovery Package "How‑to" Hub for SAP Cloud ERP, Private
entry.content:
Hello @bettina_knauss ,

OK but the S/4HANA fully-packaged on premise was delivered with the ADS, and now we only have the possibility to use SAP Forms by Adobe in free (which is limited in time and print by months). So we have to pay for a service in addition although it's offer for on premise partner. I think some parts are missing in the discovery package and should be added automatically, like for a customer pce, maybe a service plan tdd for SAP Forms for example.

Regards

entry.title: Re: CDS Custom Entities
entry.content: next, please make one where we can do Create/Update operation.

entry.title: Re: CDS Custom Entities
entry.content:
Hi @YogiPavan ,

> can't be accessed using ABAP SQL

is there something planed? 

We would like to make one or another function module available via ABAP SQL. I know that these aren’t database artefacts and that performance is poorer, but there are function modules that contain so much logic and customisation that creating a separate view would be an enormous effort.

entry.title: Re: How to use BTP destination with OAuth2ClientCredentia...
entry.content:
Hi Rayman,

You don’t necessarily need a manual CAP approach for this. The BTP Destination service can handle the token exchange automatically if configured correctly. Based on your description, here are a few things you should check:

Authentication Type: In your BTP Destination configuration, ensure the Authentication is set to OAuth2ClientCredentials. This tells the service to fetch the token using the client ID and secret before forwarding the request.

App Router Config: In your xs-app.json, make sure the route to your destination has authenticationMethod: "route".

Service Binding: The "Invalid access token" error often happens if the App Router and the Destination service are not bound to the same XSUAA instance. Check your mta.yaml to ensure both are using the same security provider.

Local vs Cloud: If you are testing via BAS preview, it sometimes struggles with token propagation. Try deploying the app to Cloud Foundry; the Managed App Router usually resolves these token issues in the live environment.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Not able to change the flexible Workflow once it is a...
entry.content:
For me the accepted answer didn't work. What worked for me was: 

  1. Find your workflow template (WS...) if you don’t know it.
  2. Run SWETYPV, filter:
    • Object Type: YOUR_ABAP_CLASS
    • Event: START_WF
  3. Note the receiver workflow template (WS...).
  4. Run PFTC_CHG.
  5. Choose Workflow Template and open that WS... template.

Enter it without WS.

entry.title: Re: Data Integration in ABAP Cloud: Data Exposure using SQL services
entry.content:
Hi @SafaBahoosh ,

 

great, do you have an answer regarding the custom entities?

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition
entry.content: If a client is using both integration suite standard and advanced event mesh, upgrading to this package certainly make sense cost-wise.

entry.title: Re: Replenishment component not displayed in checkout – C...
entry.content:
Hi all,

I resolved.

I add this code

provideConfig(<CmsConfig>{
      cmsComponents: {
        CheckoutScheduleReplenishmentOrder: {
          component: CheckoutScheduleReplenishmentOrderComponent,
          guards: [CheckoutAuthGuard, CartNotEmptyGuard],
        },
      },
    }),

 in 

 

spartacus-configuration.module.ts

 

Stefania

entry.title: Re: I am facing errors in opening the Build Apps Editor.
entry.content:
Hi Ananthu,

It seems like you've already tried the standard troubleshooting steps (cache, incognito, and boosters). Since you mentioned a "Backend Database" error earlier, it’s possible there is a sync issue between your Subaccount and the Build Apps tenant.

Before concluding it's a platform-wide outage, could you please check these two specific things:

Role Collection Assignment: Sometimes just assigning the role isn't enough. Ensure you have the BuildAppsAdmin (for development) and RegistryAdmin (for backend) role collections. Try removing and re-assigning them to your user, then log out and log back in to refresh the session.

Custom Identity Provider (IAS): Are you using a custom IAS or the default SAP ID Service? If it's a custom IdP, ensure that the "Attributes" (like email and groups) are being passed correctly in the SAML assertion, as Build Apps is very sensitive to user attributes.

Check Service Status: You can check the SAP Trust Center to see if there is an active incident for SAP Build Apps in your specific region.

If the issue persists despite these checks, it might indeed be a backend synchronization error on the SAP side, and raising a support ticket under the component CA-LCA would be the best next step.

Good luck!

entry.title: Re: Test Data Refresh service request on SAP for Me
entry.content:
Hello Tobias Koebler / TDR team,

SAP recommends to plan the TDR 'outside business hours' (to avoid data inconsistencies, which we had after our previous TDR). But the TDR starts on Monday / Tuesday and SAP takes 24 hours to refresh the data. So how can this be 'outside business hours'?

Our question: Is the selected start time exactly the time that PRD activity should be minimized to avoid data corruption? And how long will it take?  

SAP reply (S/4HANA Cloud Operations): Yes. As per best practices, we need 24 hours window to complete the data transfer. But if the data transfer completed earlier, we will update the same to you.

As companies usually do not close for 24 hours during week days the advice of planning 'outside business hours' does not make sense.  Please advise!

Thank you, Boris

entry.title: Re: Week 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account enabled with Joule for Developers
entry.content: I hope I am not late. I am not able to create the package as it hangs in this screen even after clicking 'Next' multiple times.

entry.content: @Lakshmipathi Can you give further comments?

Thanks for the tip; I tried this approach years ago, but unfortunately it didn’t work. One more reason to give it another try.
